edges.evidence_quote: The Colombo crime family ... is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the "Five Families" that dominate organized crime in New York City within the criminal organization known as the American Mafia.
edges.context: Colombo family is a constituent part of the American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family
edges.context: Infobox lists Bonanno crime family as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Colombo supporters led by Carmine Persico eventually won the second war after the exiling of the remaining Gallo crew to the Genovese family in 1975.
edges.context: Gallo crew was exiled to the Genovese family, indicating a transfer of members.

edges.evidence_quote: Magliocco joined a plot by Bonanno family boss Joseph Bonanno to assassinate the three other bosses on the Commission to take control of the New York Mafia.
edges.context: Bonanno family plotted with Colombo family boss Magliocco to assassinate other Commission bosses.

edges.evidence_quote: a meeting was called on December 5 at the Statler Hotel in Cleveland, Ohio, which was considered neutral territory under the protection and control of the Porrello crime family. Attendees representing Brooklyn included Profaci, Magliocco...
edges.context: Porrello family hosted a meeting attended by Profaci (founder of Colombo family).

edges.evidence_quote: To finally resolve the conflict, Brancato and the bosses of the other Five Families negotiated an agreement in which Albert Gallo and his remaining crew left the Colombo family and peacefully joined the Genovese family.
edges.context: The Gallo crew was originally part of the Colombo family but later left and joined the Genovese family.

edges.evidence_quote: Albert Gallo and his remaining crew left the Colombo family and peacefully joined the Genovese family.
edges.context: The Gallo crew joined the Genovese family after leaving the Colombo family.

edges.evidence_quote: DiBella was unable to prevent the Gambino family from chipping away at Colombo rackets, and the Colombos declined in power.
edges.context: The Gambino family encroached on Colombo rackets, causing the Colombos to decline in power.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2002, with the help of Bonanno family boss Joseph Massino, the other families finally allowed the Colombos to rejoin the Commission.
edges.context: The Bonanno family helped the Colombo family rejoin the Commission.

edges.evidence_quote: Lucchese underboss Anthony Casso proposed to merge the family with his own to end the war.
edges.context: The Lucchese family proposed merging with the Colombo family to end the internal war.

edges.evidence_quote: The Colombo crime family ... is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the "Five Families" that dominate organized crime in New York City within the criminal organization known as the American Mafia.
edges.context: Colombo family is a constituent part of the American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Magliocco joined a plot by Bonanno family boss Joseph Bonanno to assassinate the three other bosses on the Commission to take control of the New York Mafia.
edges.context: Colombo family boss Magliocco allied with Bonanno family in a plot to assassinate other bosses.

edges.evidence_quote: Donofrio was recorded on a wiretap speaking with capo Anthony "Big Anthony" Russo about an effort by the Colombo family to extort the Gambino family in order to pay medical expenses for family associate Walter Samperi, who was stabbed by a Gambino associate in 2008.
edges.context: Colombo family attempted to extort the Gambino family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Colombo crime family divided into two factions during the third family war (1991 to 1993).
edges.context: The two factions were in conflict during the third Colombo family war.

edges.evidence_quote: The Colombo crime family ... is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the "Five Families" that dominate organized crime in New York City within the criminal organization known as the American Mafia.
edges.context: The Colombo family is a constituent part of the American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: the gang run by Profaci became recognized as the Profaci crime family
edges.context: The Colombo family traces its roots to the Profaci crime family, which was its predecessor.

edges.evidence_quote: Magliocco joined a plot by Bonanno family boss Joseph Bonanno to assassinate the three other bosses on the Commission
edges.context: The Colombo family (under Magliocco) cooperated with the Bonanno family in a plot to assassinate other bosses.

edges.evidence_quote: Induisi was placed at a meeting with the Colombo family, with Carmine Persico, Tom DiBella, Vic Orena, John Matera, Gennaro Langella, Allie Persico and Vic Regina, and from the DeCavalcante crime family, Giovanni Riggi, Sam DeCavalcante, Stefano Vitabile, and Jimmy Rotondo.
edges.context: Members of the Colombo and DeCavalcante families attended a meeting together.

edges.evidence_quote: In February 2002, DeRoss was convicted of extortion, after Genovese family associate turncoat Michael D'Urso taped DeRoss confessing to conspiracy of obtaining 'protection money' from two deli supply companies
edges.context: A Genovese family associate provided evidence against a Colombo member, indicating interaction but not necessarily cooperation or conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: In February 1974, Aloi was sentenced by former U.S. District Judge Whitman Knapp to 9 years in prison for stock-fraud, along with Johnny Dio, an infamous member of the Lucchese family.
edges.context: A Colombo member was convicted alongside a Lucchese member for stock fraud, suggesting joint criminal activity.

edges.evidence_quote: On September 29, 2011, Fusco pleaded guilty to running a shakedown scheme against the Gambino family; he was sentenced to four months in prison.
edges.context: A Colombo member ran a shakedown scheme against the Gambino family, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: But a week later spotted in a social club ran by the Bonanno acting boss Vincent "Vinny T.V." Badalamenti.
edges.context: A Colombo soldier was seen at a social club run by a Bonanno acting boss, suggesting association but not specifying cooperation or conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Michael organized a highly lucrative gasoline scam racket with the Russian mafia.
edges.context: A Colombo member organized a gasoline scam with the Russian mafia, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: Nicholas "Nicky" Bianco — a Gallo crew member, Bianco later joined the Patriarca crime family.
edges.context: A Colombo crew member later joined the Patriarca family.

edges.evidence_quote: Gerard Pappa — a family associate who transferred to become a soldier in the Genovese crime family
edges.context: A Colombo associate transferred to become a soldier in the Genovese family.

edges.evidence_quote: Michael "Mike Rizzi" Rizzitello — a Gallo crew member, later joined the Los Angeles crime family.
edges.context: A Colombo crew member later joined the Los Angeles family.

edges.evidence_quote: Irish mobster Jimmy Coonan became Stein's bodyguard. Coonan and the Westies gang murdered Stein in 1977
edges.context: The Westies murdered a Colombo associate.

edges.evidence_quote: He first became acquainted with Lucchese crime family underboss Anthony Casso.
edges.context: A Colombo soldier became acquainted with a Lucchese underboss.

edges.evidence_quote: In December 2006, he was arrested alongside 12 other mobsters from the Colombo and Gambino crime families
edges.context: Members of both families were arrested together in a joint investigation.

edges.evidence_quote: He and his older brother were accused of planning Hector Pagan's murder, a Bonanno crime family associate
edges.context: A Colombo member planned to murder a Bonanno associate.

edges.evidence_quote: Garfield Boys – was an Italian American street gang that operated in South Brooklyn sections of Red Hook and Gowanus. The gang was headed by future Colombo boss Carmine Persico from the 1950s to the early 1970s.
edges.context: The Garfield Boys were headed by a future Colombo boss, indicating a predecessor or early form.
